---
aliases:
  - geolocator-intro.html
---

# Create

This tutorial explains how to create a GeoLocator Data Package (GeoLocator DP) from a GeoPressureTemplate project, upload it to Zenodo, and validate the draft before publication.

::: callout-note
For an introduction to this format, visit the [GeoLocator DP website](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoLocator-DP/).
:::


## Install GeoLocatoR
<a href="https://github.com/rafnuss/geolocator"><img src="https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoLocatoR/logo.png" align="right" height="139" alt="GeoLocatoR website" style="padding-left:5px;" /></a>

We use the [GeoLocatoR](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoLocatoR) package to create and validate the data package.

```{r 15-install-geolocator}
#| eval: false
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pkg_install("Rafnuss/GeoLocatoR")
```

```{r 15-setup}
#| message: false
devtools::load_all("~/Documents/GitHub/GeoLocatoR/")
# library(GeoLocatoR)
```

## Step 1. Create the package from GeoPressureTemplate

Read your project and build a geolocator-dp object.

```{r 15-read-geopressuretemplate}
#| message: false
pkg <- read_geopressuretemplate()
```

By default, `read_geopressuretemplate()` reads `data/interim/*.RData` first and falls back to raw data for tags without interim files.

- Use `from` to force a specific source (`"interim"` or `"raw"`).
- Prefix a file/folder with `_` to exclude a tag temporarily.

Inspect the package summary:

```{r 15-inspect-pkg-summary}
#| collapse: true
print(pkg)
```

## Step 2. Complete `tags` and `observations`

At this point, `tags` and `observations` will certaintly still be missing key metadata (for example ringing information).

The easiest way to fix this is to export the tables, edit them manually and then save them back.

```{r 15-export-tags-observations}
#| eval: false
write.csv(tags(pkg), file = "data/tags.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(observations(pkg), file = "data/observations.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

Rebuild and validate after editing:

```{r 15-validate-gldp-local}
#| collapse: true
pkg <- read_geopressuretemplate()
validate_gldp(pkg)
```

## Step 3. Run visual checks

In this final stage, we check the coverage, tag count, and deployment/retrieval consistency to avoid outliers or missing information. To do so, plot each variable and check that (1) all the ring numbers are present, (2) all measurements are correct and no information is missing and (3) trajectories are correct.

```{r 15-plot-pkg-coverage}
#| message: false
plot(pkg, "ring")
plot(pkg, "coverage")
plot(pkg, "map")
```

## Step 4. Write the package files

Now that we have checked the data, we can export `pkg` into the `csv` file that will be uploaded on Zenodo. 

```{r 15-write-gldp}
#| eval: false
write_gldp(pkg, "data/datapackage")
```

## Step 5. Create the Zenodo draft record

[Create a new draft on Zenodo](https://zenodo.org/uploads/new), complete all metadata fields, BUT, do not submit it just yet.

::: callout-warning
## Mandatory checklist

Complete **all** items below. If one or more are missing, the record may be returned for revision and not accepted in the community.

- [ ] Select the community: GeoLocator Data Package
- [ ] Upload all files
- [ ] Reserve a DOI
- [ ] Set resource type to Dataset
- [ ] Add a title starting with "GeoLocator Data Package:"
- [ ] Add all data package authors (with roles when possible)
- [ ] Add a basic description
- [ ] Add funding and related resources
- [ ] Add the GitHub link of the GeoPressureTemplate used
- [ ] Add any additional metadata you want to include

Review the [GeoLocator DP curation policy](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoLocator-DP/curation) before submission.

:::


## Step 6. Validate the Zenodo draft

Before submitting, read the draft back and validate exactly what Zenodo contains.

::: callout-info
Reading a draft requires a Zenodo API token. Create one at [Zenodo token settings](https://zenodo.org/account/settings/applications/tokens/new/) and save it in your keyring:

```{r 15-set-zenodo-token}
#| eval: false
keyring::key_set_with_value("ZENODO_TOKEN", password = "{your_zenodo_token}")
```
:::

```{r 15-read-zenodo-draft}
#| collapse: true
pkg <- read_zenodo(
  "10.5072/zenodo.472302",
  draft = TRUE, # Draft record
  sandbox = TRUE # Remove if using production Zenodo
)
```

::: callout-important
Run the official validation check on the draft package:

```{r 15-validate-gldp-zenodo}
#| collapse: true
validate_gldp(pkg)
```

If validation reports errors, fix the metadata/files and run the check again until valid.
:::

## Step 7. Submit 

Once all checks are valid and metadata are complete, submit the Zenodo record! 🎉 
A curator will review the data and get back to you if any revision is required.
